content.problemStatement:
Chef has two positive integers XXX and YYY. Now Chef wants to perform some number of operations (possibly zero) on them. In each operation, Chef can choose either XXX or YYY and increment it by 111. Find the minimum number of operations Chef needs to perform so that there is a positive integer strictly greater than 111 which divides both XXX and YYY (In other words, the greatest common divisor of XXX and YYY should be greater than 111).


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of operations Chef needs to perform.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: The greatest common divisor of 444 and 161616 is 444 which is already greater than 111, so Chef will not perform any operations.
Test Case 2: Chef will perform one operation and add 111 to 555555. Now the greatest common divisor of 444 and 565656 is 444 which is greater than 111.
